text: All Aboard the Data Train: HARTING DPPs incl. PCFs and Traceability Topics on Track with Railway-X gy Project Continuation? HARTING . w Q@ J J We suggest to continue with the project as planned. Pushing Performance Since 1945 < Way Forward & Success Factors Reflection & Risks = ) What are our next steps? How will we achieve this? What is important to us? ) What is our current POV on the project? Which obstacles & risks do we see? 1 Extension of AAS creation process Reflection 1 Productive generation of PCN sub models 1 Successfully demonstrated DPPs with sustainability data sharing, paving the way for O Exemplary generation of sub models for railway fire safety declarations transparent and interoperable product information in the Railway-X dataspace 0 Established standardized data models and interfaces, enabling seamless integration and future scalability — Scaling of AAS infrastructure components 1 Fostered innovation in circular business models and digital services, setting a strong 1 Stable operation of the EDC foundation for sustainable and data-driven value creation Risks — First cross-company data exchange via EDC (HARTING > HOPPECKE/SMO) 1 EDC'’s operability may hinder data integration and process automation 1 EDC'’s suitability for productive use in industrial context is uncertain 1 Reliance on software components from external research projects raises concerns about long term resilience and IT security in productive context Page 32 Railway-X | Milestone Meeting | February 2026 @ Railway_x
